violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the (front dining ), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors.Per PIC, a work order was submitted repairs are to take place the end of August 2025. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was (-25 ) parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for (chlorine ) sanitizer is (50-100 ) ppm.PIC refrilled bucket to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
Utensils were observed stored in between prep tables between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
Inspector observed a leak at the ceiling vents/fans in walk in cooler.PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cheese) in the cold-hold drawers. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC rearranged. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Cheese, chicken, and cut tomatoes were observed held between 45-65 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in walk-in freezer for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
ROP fish was observed in the walk-in cooler. Product temperature was observed above 38 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

The door to the outside, located at the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025
The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was Correct By: 08/04/2025
Lowboy and prep unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
Inspector observed leak at pipes below ice storage bin. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025

The following food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of food and debris: - slicer, - can opener, - food processor The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Per interview of staff, blenders are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. PIC notified. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Various items were observed held between 52-54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in walk-in freezer for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, pasta salad was placed warm in the walk-in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed on a sheet pan to cool in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

The door to the outside, located in the at the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025
Inspector observed an insect control device in a food prep area (above a blender). PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Eggs were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was 25 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for chlorine sanitizer is between 50-100 ppm. PIC refilled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine AND plastic wrap holder have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Inspector observed leak at pipes below 3 bay sink and ice storage bin. PIC placed a service call. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2025
The floors facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025
Inspector observed cardboard with buildup of debris being used as a floor mat in walk-in cooler and freezers. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored throughout the establishment. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2025

Handwashing sink in kitchen area was lacking handwashing signage. A sign or poster that notifies FOOD EMPLOYEES to wash their hands shall be provided at all HANDWASHING SINKS. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
Bucket of water were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Box of bagel and bucket of individually packed chicken were observed stored on the floor in the walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
Prep unit gaskets located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Observed torn. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the (3 bay sink). New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
